facts.label: THE ALL WHITES ARE BORN
facts.story: New Zealand's run to Spain 1982 included a 13-0 demolition of Fiji, with captain Steve Sumner scoring six times — a World Cup qualifying record at the time. The nickname "All Whites" had been coined a year earlier, when a commentator spotted the team's plain white kit and borrowed the joke from the All Blacks. By the time they reached Málaga, the name had stuck for good, and Oceania had its first-ever World Cup finalist.

facts.label: SMELTZ STUNS THE CHAMPIONS
facts.story: Seven minutes into their second group match in 2010, Shane Smeltz turned home a driven ball to put New Zealand ahead of reigning champions Italy — the tournament's lowest-ranked team shocking the Azzurri. Vincenzo Iaquinta's penalty pulled Italy level before halftime, but the All Whites held on for a 1-1 draw, a result that helped send Italy out of the group stage for the first time since 1974.

facts.label: REID'S STOPPAGE-TIME HEADER
facts.story: In their 2010 World Cup opener, New Zealand trailed Slovakia 1-0 deep into stoppage time when Shane Smeltz's cross found defender Winston Reid, who glanced a header past the goalkeeper in the third minute of injury time. The goal secured New Zealand's first-ever World Cup point and set the tone for a tournament in which the All Whites became the only team to leave South Africa unbeaten.

1982.headline: The All Whites Arrive

annotations.label: SUMNER AND WOODDIN STRIKE BACK
annotations.story: Down 3-0 inside 32 minutes to Scotland in their World Cup debut, New Zealand refused to fold. Captain Steve Sumner headed home in the 54th minute and Steve Wooddin added a second at 65', turning a rout into a respectable 5-2 defeat and giving Oceania football its first-ever World Cup goals.

annotations.label: BRAZIL CLOSE THE BOOK
annotations.story: New Zealand's finals ended as feared: a 3-0 loss to the Soviet Union in Málaga, then a 4-0 defeat in Seville to a rampant Brazil side cruising toward the second round. Three games, three losses, twelve goals conceded — but a first World Cup campaign complete all the same.

annotations.label: THE TEAL ALTERNATE
annotations.story: Adidas supplied the all-white home kit that gave the All Whites their name, plus a rarely seen teal-and-navy pinstriped away shirt from the same qualifying era — worn in a 5-0 rout of Saudi Arabia. In Spain, though, New Zealand never needed the alternate: white sufficed for all three group matches.

1982.homeColors: white shirt with black V-neck collar, black cuffs, and three black shoulder stripes (adidas)
1982.awayColors: teal shirt with black V-neck collar, black cuffs, and two black shoulder stripes (adidas)

2010.headline: The Unbeaten Underdogs

annotations.label: REID RESCUES A POINT
annotations.story: Trailing Slovakia 1-0 deep into stoppage time in their World Cup opener, New Zealand won a corner. Shane Smeltz's cross found Winston Reid, who glanced a header into the bottom corner in the 93rd minute — New Zealand's first-ever World Cup goal and point, sending the Royal Bafokeng crowd into disbelief.

annotations.label: SMELTZ FLOORS THE CHAMPIONS
annotations.story: Seven minutes into their second group game, Shane Smeltz poked New Zealand ahead of defending champions Italy. Vincenzo Iaquinta's penalty leveled it before the break, but the All Whites held on for a 1-1 draw — a result that left Italy needing a win in their final match just to survive.

annotations.label: UNBEATEN AND OUT
annotations.story: A goalless draw with Paraguay in Polokwane completed New Zealand's group stage: three draws, three points, and the only unbeaten record of the entire 2010 tournament. It wasn't enough for the knockout rounds, but it turned the All Whites into the World Cup's most unlikely feel-good story.

2010.homeColors: white shirt with black Nike swoosh, silver fern crest, and grey-striped cuffs (Nike)
2010.awayColors: black shirt with white Nike swoosh, white fern crest, and white sleeve stripes (Nike)

2026.headline: All Whites Go All Black
2026.homeColors: black shirt with an all-over silver fern graphic, white crest, and white PUMA logo (PUMA)
2026.awayColors: white shirt with a pale swirling pattern inspired by Ngā Hau e Whā (the four winds), black crest and side panels (PUMA)

annotations.label: THE KEY MOMENT
annotations.story: Elijah Just scored twice against Iran, both times combining with veteran captain Chris Wood, to become the first-ever Motherwell player to find the net at a men's World Cup. His second goal, ten minutes into the second half, salvaged a 2-2 draw — the All Whites' only point of a tough Group G campaign.

annotations.label: THE KIT
annotations.story: For the first time since the nickname was born in 1982, the All Whites didn't wear white at home. PUMA's 2026 kit went all black, wrapped in an oversized silver fern print, with the away shirt flipping to white with a swirling pattern inspired by Ngā Hau e Whā, the four winds of Māori tradition.

annotations.label: THE CONTEXT
annotations.story: Chris Wood and Tommy Smith, both part of New Zealand's unbeaten 2010 squad in South Africa, returned sixteen years later as the only players in the country's history to appear at two World Cups. Wood, at 34, captained a squad where half the group had passed 25 caps — a rare case of experience outweighing youth for an Oceania side.
